evidence.velocity_vs_rigour: Build scalable data transformations and automated data quality processes.
evidence.velocity_vs_rigour_explanation: Repeated rigour signals — data quality, governance, reliability, testing frameworks — dominate with no velocity/speed language present.
evidence.domain_risk: Our portfolio is crucial for clinical decision-making and treatment pathways.
evidence.domain_risk_explanation: Regulated healthcare/medtech sector is a default-high domain, reinforced by data feeding clinical decision-making.
evidence.collaboration_width: Data Engineers; BI Developers; business stakeholders
evidence.data_team_maturity: bridge the gap between data engineering and analytics
evidence.data_team_maturity_explanation: Named adjacent data roles (Data Engineers, BI Developers) and an existing modern stack in responsibilities indicate a growing function rather than early-stage or fully mature.
evidence.jd_authorship: Design and develop analytics-ready data models and semantic layers in Snowflake.
evidence.jd_authorship_explanation: Named tools (Snowflake, Power BI, dbt) with precise application context make the day-to-day work fairly clear, though some bullets ('Strong communication and collaboration skills') are generic.
evidence.stakeholder_orientation: power reporting, dashboards and next-generation AI solutions
evidence.stakeholder_orientation_explanation: Primary audience is internal data consumers and self-service analytics/BI platform users, placing this in internal_data.
evidence.autonomy_level: Drive data governance, documentation and business-friendly data definitions.
evidence.autonomy_level_explanation: The role drives governance and standards (strategic) while also partnering to deliver insights for stakeholders (execution).
evidence.ai_role: power reporting, dashboards and next-generation AI solutions
evidence.ai_role_explanation: The candidate builds data models and pipelines that power AI-driven reporting and AI solutions downstream, meeting the ai_enabler pattern (AI-ready data foundations).
evidence.testing_framing: Experience developing and maintaining data pipelines, transformations and testing frameworks.
evidence.testing_framing_explanation: Testing frameworks appear in the requirements/skills list as experience rather than an explicit owned responsibility with an ownership verb.
evidence.loss_aversion_framing: Create trusted datasets and standardised KPIs that support consistent reporting.
evidence.loss_aversion_framing_explanation: High: regulated healthcare context combined with repeated 'trusted', 'single source of truth', and governance language throughout signals fear of bad data reaching clinical/business decision-makers.
evidence.greenfield_vs_fix: Improve data reliability, performance and automation across the analytics ecosystem.
evidence.greenfield_vs_fix_explanation: Mix of build/design (greenfield: 'design and develop... semantic layers') and improve/scale (fix_scale: 'improve data reliability') verbs with no dominant direction.
evidence.loss_aversion: Create trusted datasets and standardised KPIs — healthcare data feeding clinical decision-making raises the cost of inaccuracy.
